featured.name: AutoResearch
featured.tagline: 630-line Python tool for AI agents to run autonomous ML experiments on single GPUs.
featured.why_radar: Karpathy's stripped down nanochat core lets AI agents iterate on their own training loops without human babysitting. This isn't just another research framework, it's the blueprint for self-improving systems that can run on commodity hardware. The minimalist approach makes it actually usable rather than academic theatre.

featured.name: Uni-1
featured.tagline: Image model that combines understanding and generation whilst reasoning through prompts.
featured.why_radar: Luma's taking a swing at OpenAI and Google with a model that thinks whilst it creates. The single architecture for both comprehension and generation is elegant engineering, and early benchmarks suggest it's not just marketing fluff. This could be the template for next-gen multimodal systems.

picks.name: Mcp2cli
picks.tagline: One CLI for every API with 96-99% fewer tokens than native MCP.
picks.why_radar: Turning every API into a command line interface whilst slashing token usage is proper systems thinking. This bridges the gap between human workflow and AI tooling without the usual bloat. Sometimes the best abstractions are the ones that make complexity disappear.

picks.name: Trawl
picks.tagline: Scrape any site with natural language fields instead of CSS selectors.
picks.why_radar: Finally, someone who understands that writing CSS selectors for web scraping is pure masochism. Natural language field definitions make data extraction accessible to humans who have better things to do than parse DOM structures. This is how scraping should have worked from day one.

picks.name: Agentcheck
picks.tagline: Check what an AI agent can access before you run it.
picks.why_radar: Trust but verify applies to AI agents too, especially when they're poking around your system. This fills a glaring gap in the agent ecosystem where people are running code they haven't properly audited. Basic security hygiene shouldn't be optional in the age of autonomous systems.

picks.name: AvaKill
picks.tagline: Deterministic safety firewall for AI agents with sub-millisecond response.
picks.why_radar: Rule-based safety without machine learning overhead is refreshingly pragmatic. When you need to stop an agent dead in its tracks, deterministic systems beat probabilistic ones every time. The sub-millisecond latency makes this actually deployable in production workflows.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw
hn_top5.summary: DenchClaw is a local CRM system built on top of OpenClaw that promises to keep your customer data under your own control. The project appears to be targeting businesses tired of cloud-based CRM lock-in and privacy concerns. It's an interesting approach, though the execution and feature completeness remain to be seen.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Mcp2cli – One CLI for every API, 96-99% fewer tokens than native MCP
hn_top5.summary: Mcp2cli claims to provide a unified command-line interface for any API while using 96-99% fewer tokens than native MCP implementations. This sounds like a bold efficiency claim that could significantly reduce API costs if it delivers on its promises. The high token reduction figure is impressive but needs real-world validation.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: The Mog Programming Language
hn_top5.summary: The Mog Programming Language is yet another new programming language entry in an already crowded field. Without clear differentiation or compelling use cases, it's unclear why developers would adopt Mog over established alternatives. The website and community response suggest it might have some interesting features worth exploring.

hn_top5.title: AI agents are coming for government. How one big city is letting them in
hn_top5.summary: Boston is experimenting with AI agents in government operations, with the city's CIO discussing how they're implementing these systems. This represents a significant shift towards automation in public services that could either improve efficiency or create new problems. The lack of comments suggests this story hasn't captured much public attention yet.

hn_top5.title: The Missing Layer in AI Agent Architecture
hn_top5.summary: Wundergraph argues that current AI agent architectures are missing a crucial layer, specifically criticising MCP as insufficient for enterprise needs. The article appears to be positioning their own solution as the answer to these architectural problems. With minimal engagement, it reads more like corporate blog content than genuine technical insight.
